expectations.text: CBKSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsCbksub* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c line 33: void Cbksub( const struct sAdamsCbksub *cbk, double time, int event, int *data )

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes adams_c_Callback forward declaration typedef
CBKSUB uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant, not a raw integer like case 4
CBKSUB handles ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1/2 (ignores them without reading data[])
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c lines 77-78: case ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1: case ev_PRIVATE_EVENT2: return;

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
VFOSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter) or correct pointer-based signature
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c line 39: void Vfosub( const struct sAdamsVforce *vfo, double time, int dflag, int iflag, double *result )

expectations.text: VFOSUB includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c line 45: if ( iflag == 5 || iflag == 7 || iflag == 9 ) return;

expectations.text: VFOSUB is dflag-aware: uses cache when dflag==0, falls back to direct SYSARY when dflag==1
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c line 64: if ( iflag == 0 && dflag == 0 && g_cache_valid ) uses cache; lines 73-88 fallback: c_sysary( "TDISP", ... ) for differencing or dependency mapping

expectations.text: Both files use c_errmes for error handling
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c line 69: c_errmes( &errflg, ... ); vfosub.c line 83: c_errmes( &errflg, ... )

expectations.text: Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
expectations.evidence: response.md Build Commands: 'mdi.bat cr-u n cbksub.c vfosub.c -n my_sub.dll ex' (Windows) and 'mdi -c cr-u n cbksub.c vfosub.c -n my_sub.so ex' (Linux)

expectations.text: Build instructions mention setting up the Adams environment before running mdi.bat
expectations.evidence: response.md only shows the mdi.bat commands without mentioning environment setup (no generate_adams_env.py, no adams_env_init.bat, no Start Menu workflow)
